arts.ttl: Hull City's Championship run-in examined as Tigers look to win relegation battle
arts.sum: City kick-off their run-in with the first of five home games on Wednesday night when they welcome fellow strugglers Oxford United to the MKM Stadium, looking to secure successive home wins for the first time this season, and in fact, since November 2023 when Liam Rosenior's side won three on the spin. After the victory over Plymouth ended City's long wait for a home win, fans will arrive at the MKM Stadium on Wednesday night with a little less fear, and a bit more hope and expectation.

arts.ttl: Dutch defender hopes to have done enough after first league start under Gary Rowett
arts.sum: HIDDE ter Avest hopes he has done enough to retain his place in the Oxford United line-up for the trip to Hull City tonight.

arts.ttl: Crooks back in: Hull City team news and predicted XI to face Oxford United
arts.sum: Hull City are back in Championship action on Wednesday night with a home clash against Oxford United at… The post Crooks back in: Hull City team news and predicted XI to face Oxford United appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Ruben Selles' Hull City influence growing as telling impact about to emerge
arts.sum: There would have been no shortage of pride at what turned out to be one of the most resolute, hard-working and battling displays of any team in the Championship this season, one full of grit and determination to not be beaten after losing Joao Pedro with not even 15 minutes played in the Ashton Gate sunshine. Given City's wretched record on the red side of the Bristol which has seen them win one league game in 60 years, and lose the last five, there was probably an element of relief it wasn't any kind of defeat, let alone a battling/brave one.

arts.ttl: Lewie Coyle keen to keep Hull City momentum ahead of 'big' Oxford United clash
arts.sum: Boss Ruben Selles will now look to other members of his attacking unit to conjure up the goals to see City come through the next three games, starting with the visit of Oxford United on Wednesday night, before facing West Brom at The Hawthorns on Saturday, followed by the home game against Luton Town after the international break. Lewie Coyle has praised the resolve and determination of Hull City in the past two games to claim four points from six against Plymouth Argyle and Bristol City as they look to gradually move closer to safety.

arts.ttl: How Hull City's final 10 fixtures compare to relegation rivals Derby County, Stoke City and Luton
arts.sum: Luton Town and Plymouth Argyle both suffered heavy defeats, but there was a first win in 2025 for Derby County against John Eustace's former club, Derby County, while Portsmouth moved 10 points clear of danger on Sunday with a fine win over leaders Leeds United. City's point at Ashton Gate kept the Tigers five points clear of danger ahead of their big home meeting with Oxford United on Tuesday night, but the manner of their resolute, battling display will give them more confidence going into the final 10 games of the season.
